title: suregada subglomerata
intro: Suregada subglomerata, also known as the Indian cork tree or wild rubber, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots contain compounds with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may provide relief from pain, reduce swelling, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of Suregada subglomerata include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations.
